about.metaDescription: Sander de Snaijer is a frontend developer with 10+ years of experience at companies like KNMI, Bynder and Dutch government. Based in the Netherlands.

blog.metaDescription: Tutorials and write-ups on frontend development, Flutter, Next.js, and maker projects like 3D printing and Arduino. Written by Sander de Snaijer.

project.metaDescription: Explore side projects by Sander de Snaijer — webcam games, Flutter apps, Arduino clocks, and 3D-printed experiments, all built in spare time.

home.title: Sander de Snaijer — Frontend Developer | React, TypeScript, Next.js
home.metaDescription: Frontend developer from the Netherlands building React apps, side projects, and unusual things with 3D printers. Projects, tutorials, and blog posts.
home.introLead: Hi, I'm Sander — a frontend developer from the Netherlands with a thing for building things that shouldn't exist. By day I write React and TypeScript at <knmi>KNMI</knmi> (the Dutch weather institute). By night I experiment: face-controlled Tetris, 3D-printed stained glass, Flutter apps, and webcam duck hunts.
home.introClosing: This site is where I document the experiments, write about what I learn, and show what I've built.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Explore projects and articles by topic.
tags.metaDescription: Discover projects and articles organized by topic.

slugMetaDescriptions.javascript: JavaScript projects and tutorials by Sander de Snaijer: browser games, interactive tools, webcam experiments, and frontend development.
slugMetaDescriptions.typescript: TypeScript tutorials and projects by Sander de Snaijer: typed JavaScript in Next.js apps, Flutter, browser games, and open-source tools.
slugMetaDescriptions.nextjs: Next.js tutorials and projects by Sander de Snaijer: portfolio setup, headless CMS integration with Sanity, App Router, and browser game development.
slugMetaDescriptions.react: React tutorials and projects by Sander de Snaijer: Next.js apps, Redux state management, browser games, and open-source geospatial tools.

slugMetaDescriptions.css3: CSS3 tutorials and projects by Sander de Snaijer: responsive headers, dark mode, mobile-first layouts, and practical styling techniques.
slugMetaDescriptions.3d-printing: 3D printing projects and guides by Sander de Snaijer: word clocks, resin stained glass, silicone molds, and maker experiments with Bambu X1C.

slugMetaDescriptions.android: Android app development projects by Sander de Snaijer: Flutter apps on Google Play, cross-platform builds with Dart, and Play Store publishing guides.

slugMetaDescriptions.tailwind-css: Tailwind CSS projects and notes by Sander de Snaijer: utility-first styling in Next.js, dark mode support, and responsive layout techniques.
slugMetaDescriptions.html: HTML tutorials and projects by Sander de Snaijer: semantic markup, accessible structure, and responsive web layouts from scratch.
slugMetaDescriptions.webcam: Webcam browser projects by Sander de Snaijer: face-controlled Tetris, hand-tracked Duck Hunt, and experiments using MediaPipe in the browser.

slugMetaDescriptions.c: C++ Arduino projects by Sander de Snaijer: firmware for word clocks and LED hardware, written by a frontend developer stepping into embedded code.
slugMetaDescriptions.redux: Redux projects by Sander de Snaijer: state management in React apps, including large enterprise dashboards and open-source geospatial tools.
slugMetaDescriptions.three-js: Three.js browser projects by Sander de Snaijer: 3D WebGL rendering in Next.js, including an interactive toilet paper roll physics simulator.
slugMetaDescriptions.firebase: Firebase project by Sander de Snaijer: backend integration for Sudoku Ultimato, a cross-platform Flutter app for iOS and Android.
slugMetaDescriptions.storybook: Storybook project by Sander de Snaijer: component-driven development for the OpenGeoWeb weather mapping platform, built with React and TypeScript.
slugMetaDescriptions.jest: Jest testing projects by Sander de Snaijer: unit tests for React components and TypeScript utilities in Next.js and enterprise frontend apps.
slugMetaDescriptions.cypress: Cypress testing by Sander de Snaijer: end-to-end browser tests for React and Next.js apps, covering navigation, layout, and dynamic content.
slugMetaDescriptions.resin: Resin crafting project by Sander de Snaijer: a step-by-step guide to making stained glass effects using coloured resin and 3D printed frames.
slugMetaDescriptions.arts-and-crafts: Arts and crafts projects by Sander de Snaijer: handmade creations using 3D printing and resin, blending technology with traditional craft techniques.
slugMetaDescriptions.localization: Flutter localization guide by Sander de Snaijer: adding multi-language support to iOS and Android apps, including Dutch and English for Sudoku Ultimato.
slugMetaDescriptions.silicone-molds: Silicone mold project by Sander de Snaijer: how to cast food-safe silicone molds from 3D printed masters and use them to make custom chocolate bars.
slugMetaDescriptions.3d-modelling: 3D modelling projects by Sander de Snaijer: designing parts in Fusion 360 for 3D printing, including molds, clock housings, and custom enclosures.
slugMetaDescriptions.headless-cms: Headless CMS guide by Sander de Snaijer: setting up Sanity with Next.js for a developer portfolio, including schemas, queries, and Vercel deployment.
slugMetaDescriptions.app-development: Mobile app development by Sander de Snaijer: building Flutter apps for iOS and Android as a frontend web developer, with honest lessons from the process.
slugMetaDescriptions.electronics: Electronics maker project by Sander de Snaijer: building an Arduino word clock with soldering, LED wiring, and custom C++ firmware in a 3D printed case.

slugIntros.javascript: Projects and posts using JavaScript: from browser games with webcam controls to interactive tools and frontend experiments.
slugIntros.typescript: TypeScript projects and write-ups: safer JavaScript for frontend apps, games, and tools, with real examples from production and side projects.
slugIntros.nextjs: Next.js projects and tutorials: this portfolio, the Sudoku Ultimato site, browser games, and guides on the App Router, Sanity CMS, and deployment.
slugIntros.react: React projects and posts: from large-scale enterprise apps to side projects, games, and portfolio work. Component architecture, hooks, and real-world patterns.
slugIntros.flutter: Flutter projects and tutorials: building cross-platform iOS and Android apps as a frontend web developer, including a Sudoku app and a Tabata timer.
slugIntros.figma: Figma in practice: designing UI components, mobile layouts, and full product flows before writing a single line of code.
slugIntros.css3: CSS projects and tutorials: responsive layouts, scroll effects, dark mode, and component styling without reaching for a framework.
slugIntros.3d-printing: 3D printing projects: Arduino word clocks, stained glass with resin, chocolate bar molds, and other experiments printed on a Bambu X1C.
slugIntros.dart: Dart projects: the language behind Flutter, used to build cross-platform mobile apps for iOS and Android including Sudoku Ultimato.
slugIntros.ios: iOS app development with Flutter: building, testing, and shipping to the App Store as a developer coming from a web background.
slugIntros.android: Android development with Flutter: shipping to Google Play, handling multi-language support, and building cross-platform apps from a single codebase.
slugIntros.diy: DIY projects where code meets craft: 3D printed molds, resin casting, Arduino builds, and things that are more fun to make than to buy.
slugIntros.makers: Maker projects: hardware, 3D printing, soldering, and hands-on builds. The place where software instincts meet physical materials.
slugIntros.tailwind-css: Tailwind CSS in practice: utility-first styling for Next.js apps, dark mode, responsive design, and why the long class names are worth it.
slugIntros.html: HTML projects and tutorials: semantic markup, accessibility, and the foundation beneath every project on this site.
slugIntros.webcam: Webcam-powered browser projects: face tracking, hand detection, and games controlled by nothing but a camera and a bit of MediaPipe magic.
slugIntros.mediapipe: MediaPipe in the browser: real-time face and hand tracking used to build webcam-controlled games without any server-side processing.
slugIntros.webgl: WebGL browser experiments: 3D rendering and graphics in the browser, used in webcam games and interactive visual projects.
slugIntros.arduino: Arduino projects: hardware builds combining microcontrollers, 3D-printed enclosures, and custom firmware, like a word clock with minute-level accuracy.
slugIntros.c: C++ for hardware projects: writing firmware for Arduino microcontrollers to drive LED matrices, timekeeping, and physical devices.
slugIntros.redux: Redux state management: used in large-scale React applications at companies like Bynder, KNMI, and Dutch government projects.
slugIntros.three-js: Three.js in the browser: 3D WebGL rendering used to build the Toilet Paper Roll Simulator, a surprisingly delightful physics experiment.
slugIntros.firebase: Firebase used as a backend for Sudoku Ultimato: handling real-time data, user state, and analytics for a cross-platform mobile app.
slugIntros.storybook: Storybook for component development: used in the OpenGeoWeb project to develop and document React UI components in isolation.
slugIntros.jest: Jest for unit testing: testing React components and utility functions, used across multiple client projects and open-source tools.
slugIntros.cypress: Cypress for end-to-end testing: browser automation tests for React apps, ensuring navigation, forms, and dynamic content work as expected.
slugIntros.resin: Resin casting projects: using coloured UV resin together with 3D printed frames to create stained glass artwork without cutting real glass.
slugIntros.arts-and-crafts: Arts and crafts projects where technology meets handcraft: 3D printed frames, resin pours, and things that live somewhere between making and building.
slugIntros.localization: Localization in Flutter: adding multi-language support to iOS and Android apps, covering string management, locale detection, and App Store requirements.
slugIntros.silicone-molds: Silicone mold making: casting food-safe molds from 3D printed masters to create custom chocolate bars with any logo or shape.
slugIntros.3d-modelling: 3D modelling for makers: designing printable parts in Fusion 360, from chocolate bar molds to word clock housings.
slugIntros.headless-cms: Headless CMS with Sanity: separating content from code in a Next.js portfolio, with custom schemas, real-time previews, and structured content.
slugIntros.app-development: App development from a web developer's perspective: building and shipping cross-platform mobile apps with Flutter, covering the full journey from idea to store.
slugIntros.electronics: Electronics projects: soldering, wiring, and building hardware-powered devices, like an Arduino word clock with an LED matrix and custom firmware.

consent.message: We use cookies to track statistics, no personal data

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This website, sanderdesnaijer.com, uses cookies to ensure the site works properly and to understand how it is used.

whatAreCookies.title: What are cookies
whatAreCookies.description: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help websites function and provide basic information about usage.

howCookiesAreUsed.title: How cookies are used
howCookiesAreUsed.description: This website uses the following types of cookies:

essentialCookies.title: Essential cookies
essentialCookies.description: These are required for the website to function correctly, such as remembering your cookie preferences and ensuring basic functionality.

analyticsCookies.title: Analytics cookies (optional)
analyticsCookies.description: These may be used to understand how visitors use the website, such as which pages are visited and how long users stay. This helps improve the website and projects shared here. These cookies are only used with your consent where required.

thirdPartyServices.title: Third-party services
thirdPartyServices.description: Some pages may include content from third-party platforms such as GitHub or YouTube. These services may set their own cookies when you interact with them.

yourChoices.title: Your choices
yourChoices.description: You can accept or reject non-essential cookies using the cookie banner. You can also control or delete cookies through your browser settings.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: If you have questions, you can contact:
